requirements.task_name: Create a collection of 3 deep-sea mollusk species longer than 10 cm living below 1000 m
requirements.task_description: Use advanced search and sorting to find marine mollusk species with a recorded minimum depth of 1000 m or deeper and a shell/body length of at least 10 cm, then create a collection named “Deep Sea Study” that contains exactly 3 such species selected from the top of the results list.

requirements.task_name: Select the Tridacna species with the highest conservation risk and add it to a comparison list
requirements.task_description: Find all species within the genus Tridacna, sort them by conservation status severity (from most threatened to least), select the single species with the highest risk category, and add that species to a comparison list named “High-risk clams”.

requirements.task_name: Build an ‘Invasive NA Freshwater’ collection with 2 invasive mollusks recorded after 1950
requirements.task_description: Use filters to find freshwater mollusk species that are marked as invasive and present in North America, then from those, select two species whose ‘First recorded in region’ year is 1950 or later and save them into a collection named “Invasive NA Freshwater”.

requirements.task_name: Identify the largest Indo-Pacific cephalopod (≥50 cm) and add it to a ‘Giant Cephalopods’ list
requirements.task_description: Filter cephalopod species from the Indo-Pacific region with a maximum length of at least 50 cm, sort them by maximum length, and then add the single largest species to a list named “Giant Cephalopods”.

requirements.task_name: Find a cephalopod camouflage teaching resource (ages 12–14, from 2019–2024) and favorite it
requirements.task_description: Locate an educational resource about cephalopod camouflage intended for ages 12–14 and published between 2019 and 2024, then add that single resource to your favorites list.

requirements.task_name: Compare distributions of a species found in both Japan and Australia with a Japan-only species
requirements.task_description: Use the distribution map and filters to find one mollusk species that occurs in both Japan and Australia and another species that occurs in Japan but not in Australia, then add both species to a comparison view named “Japan vs Australia”.

requirements.task_name: Bookmark the earliest described Muricidae species from the Western Atlantic
requirements.task_description: Within the family Muricidae, filter species to those occurring in the Western Atlantic, sort them by year of description from oldest to newest, select the earliest described species, and bookmark it.

requirements.task_name: Create a ‘Venomous snails’ collection with 3 species having a toxoglossan radula
requirements.task_description: Use the trait or glossary filters to find mollusk species with the radula type trait set to ‘toxoglossan’, then select the first three species from the results and save them into a collection called “Venomous snails”.
